title: kd.lab
summary: Started with a Shopify design gallery video and ended up with a live website. A full session building a public-facing experiment diary from scratch — design, infrastructure, CMS, and DNS in one sitting. The experiment documents its own blind spot: we built a mile-marking tool without mile-marking the build.

tiles.image_alt: Screenshots from the Shopify design gallery video

tiles.body: A YouTube video about Shopify's internal tool for designers to share experiments. 200+ designers, a feed of dynamic tiles, a canvas of mixed artifact types. I've always wanted something like this.
Realized mid-conversation there were actually two things: a *collaborative team tool* (like GitHub for design process) and a *personal public diary*. Tabled the team tool. Built the diary.
One experiment page. Tile-based canvas. Three tile types to start. Polished container, messy content.

tiles.image_alt: First version of the experiment page prototype

tiles.body: The header felt like a notebook — experiment number, date, tag, title with an italic word, tools pills. None of that was asked for. It just appeared and was exactly right.

Went through three iterations to get the width right. Nav pill needed to *break out* of the content column while keeping label text aligned with it. Two column widths: `--wide-width` and `--col-width`.
Lift + shadow reads as 'click me to go somewhere.' Annotation tiles don't go anywhere. Removed the lift. Only link tile arrows move.
Final artifact: solid border, settled. WIP: animated dashed border, diagonal stripe background. The *feeling* communicates before the badge does.
Started as cards. Wrong — read like more canvas tiles. Stripped to open layout on cream background, no white boxes. Lift on hover, bigger arrow nudge. Reads like the header now.

Static HTML was fine for the prototype. For 4–7 experiments a month over a year, it would become painful around experiment 10. Chose Netlify + Decap CMS + GitHub: git-backed, no server to maintain, real admin UI, auto-deploys.
One `experiment.html` reads any content JSON file. Update the design once, every experiment inherits it. The alternative — one HTML file per experiment — would have been a maintenance nightmare.
Tried to hand over the repo as a downloadable tar file. She can't download files from Claude. Ended up copy-pasting 6 files one at a time into GitHub's web UI. Very on brand.
Decap CMS at `/admin`, protected by Netlify Identity. Git Gateway connects saves to GitHub. Every experiment published through the form becomes a commit. 'New Experiment' button exists. It works.

Built a mile-marking tool without mile-marking the build. Design iterations were written over instead of versioned. The screenshots from earlier states exist only in the chat history. *The experiment documents its own problem.*

The lab-session skill was written at the end of this session specifically because of this failure. Next session: `/mark` after every meaningful state. `/artifact` for every file produced. `/wrap` generates the CMS JSON.
A− for output, C for process documentation. The site is real, the CMS works, the skill exists. The build artifacts are gone.
One full session, March 24 2026. Concept to live URL in a single sitting.
